cold_start: <1 s (docs say 'boots in under a second'; 'boot times measured in milliseconds')
isolation: Linux microVM (official docs say 'individual Linux microVMs'; Firecracker is named by third-party blogs and HN commentary but not in the official Deno documentation)
max_runtime: 30 min (per docs limits; extendable on demand via extendTimeout)
name: Deno Sandbox
notes: Snapshots capture only the filesystem (read-only images of bootable volumes) — RAM state is not preserved across pause/resume. No Docker, GPU, or automatic-wake-on-request capability is documented. Default concurrency during pre-release is 5 sandboxes per organization (raise via deploy@deno.com).
price_headline: $0.05 per CPU-hour (40h/month included with Pro plan, then $0.05/h; Free plan 15h included)
pricing_model: Billed on CPU time (and memory time) consumed, not wall-clock; included in Deno Deploy plans
summary: Deno Sandbox is a managed service on Deno Deploy that provisions short-lived, isolated Linux microVMs via an API. Each sandbox is API-driven from the @deno/sandbox SDK and is intended for running untrusted or LLM-generated code on behalf of AI agents, CI runners, plug-in systems, or vibe-coding environments. The runtime is a full Linux environment with files, processes, package managers, and background services.
